title: Monday.com Answers: How to export and print the Gantt
excerpt:
Hey guys! Many people are asking about exporting our brand new Gantt chart so I thought I would explain how to do it! 
To export the Gantt View to excel, open up the Gantt View and click the 3-dot menu at the top right-h…

posts.author: Kayla-monday.com (Kayla Kaplan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey guys! Many people are asking about exporting our brand new Gantt chart so I thought I would explain how to do it!

To export the Gantt View to excel, open up the Gantt View and click the 3-dot menu at the top right-hand corner or the view. Next, click “Export to Excel”, like we’ve done in the image below.

Tip: Make sure to click the view’s 3-dot menu, and not the board’s, which is located at the very top of the board in the upper header.

1600×750 163 KB

Now, you can print the Gantt View that you have just exported right from Excel! Currently, you cannot print the Gantt View directly from monday.com, so make sure to export it first.

To export the Gantt Widget to Excel, head over to your dashboard and click the little Excel icon on the top-left corner of the widget. Once you have exported the widget, you can print it directly from Excel.

1600×820 186 KB

posts.author: tylerrake (K)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, The Export to Excel is easy to use. But frankly, it is very poor version of Gantt compared to what we view in Monday.com The “Print Board” feature print/save Gantt to pdf but problem is it only shows top part of Gannt and if it is big Gantt chart, cannot print/save whole Gantt chart in 1 pdf file.

printboard1325×749 176 KB

posts.author: zoki (Zoran)
posts.cookedHtml:
Couldn’t agree more with the other reply.

Monday is absolutely amazing in what it can provide for your business!
Its only flaw is you cant print the Gant chart you see in the browser, I cant believe that of all the things it is the one simple task.

A client said to me after showing them Monday “its like buying a Ferrari only to pop the bonnet and find a lawn mower engine in it”

posts.author: devontrew (Devon)
posts.cookedHtml: Any feedback on this one? Seems like a massive over site to not allow customers to export to anything other than a pretty shoddy Excel. A bit disappointed that I purchased an annual license if I am being honest. Unable to prepare PPT reports for my CIO is a way that can be presented outside of Monday.com

posts.author: Creegster (Craig)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this? I havent seen a reply to the original request from May, it would be great if there was at least some sort of response this request, anything at all…

posts.author: AdamWright (Adam Wright)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi,

When I was in the trail period I was able to create a link from the Gantt chart which is a great to share with clients.

https://view.monday.com/embed/1582800869-67b54b2df312e813ecf05ae956dd3183?r=use1

I can not find this feature now. Can you please let me know what I need to do to get it back?

Thanks

posts.author: Mark_K (Mark Kargl)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Adam -

To get a sharable link to a gantt chart, hover on the “Gantt” button at the top of the page, click on the three dots (…), and select “Share settings.” In the pop-up window you should be able to copy a sharable link or the embed code.

It’s not an exported PDF like you can get from Smartsheets but it’s better than the exported Excel gantt chart.

Hope this helps.

Mark

posts.author: rexx (rexx )
posts.cookedHtml:
Why is my gannt chart so truncated and small? It is truncated in my view while I’m working on it and also when I have shared the link. I see other people’s in this thread where it is full screen, but even when I press “full screen” only changes a fraction of the size. I can view other people’s link and it is not truncated (like AdamWright’s link below.) So it’s not my browser.

Here’s an example of mine: Gantt

Please advise.

posts.author: rexx (rexx )
posts.cookedHtml:

posts.author: Alexvim (Alex Parker)
posts.cookedHtml:
Adding here the need for a aesthetically pleasing solution to a Gantt Chart export.

Exporting to excel is really not an acceptable solution.

Considering the Gantt Chart is a well established project management tool, its strange to see that Monday doesn’t offer something that we can quickly export and send to our teams.

Would love some focus on this topic from the company!

posts.author: AlichiaS (Ali Sawitoski)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on being able to export the full Gantt view to a PDF or even a PNG?

posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @AdamWright, I clicked on your link and your tasks and associated bars on the timeline look like they are much closer together than mine. Can you let me know you change the spacing?
Thanks!

posts.author: Micahmay76 (Micah May)
posts.cookedHtml: I am actually a bit embarrassed by this complete oversight on Monday’s part. I budgeted and pushed for Monday.com, not realizing there was no way to PDF or print a GANTT chart, I rarely use them. But I have users who are trying to get a workable GANTT chart and I have to tell them the only option is some kindergartners version of a GANTT chart in Excel. That Excel exported GANTT chart is horrendous and I am trying to be nice here and not say how I really feel about it. Monday you have to do better, please get this resolved as soon as possible.

posts.cookedHtml: The fact we cant have a decent viewer or a pdf print of the gantt chart unless this poor excel version is quite frankly frustrating. It hinders me the usage of this plataform almost completelly.

posts.author: YERIO (Ioannis Yerolatsitis)

posts.author: zachb (Zach Baldry)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday-team, update on having a usable gantt export?

posts.author: asnagel (Adeline Snagel)
posts.cookedHtml: My client requested an 8.5x11 pdf of a Gantt chart we had shared with her, and the fact that I cannot easily complete this simple request is ridiculous. Truly embarrassing for client communication. The enormous ‘Monday’ branded footer obscures my data, there is no way to include our company branding that is present elsewhere within our account, and the export doesn’t even include dependency lines. Seriously considering wasting my time re-entering these dates into Microsoft Project rather than send anything from Monday.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi everyone! Thank you for your patience with us as our team have worked behind the scenes to develop this particular feature. I can confirm that export to PDF feature is currently in gradual release to all accounts, with an expected full release ETA for the end of this month. You can find more information about this release via our announcements page here In development: Export Gantt to PDF, where we post all new and upcoming feature releases in the platform
